title: Lepidaploa edmundoi
intro: Lepidaploa edmundoi is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Edmund's feather-top. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been associated with the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Lepidaploa edmundoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential uses.

It belongs to the genus Lepidaploa, a group of plants that are native to the tropical regions of South America. Lepidaploa edmundoi is a relatively rare and endemic species, found primarily in the tropical forests of Brazil.
